label: Context windows & working memory

node.label: Context windows & working memory
node.definition: Temporary information is maintained inside the current sequence, recurrent state, or active workspace.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Context windows & working memory is modeled as depending on Sequences, events & time series, Self-attention and feeding downstream nodes such as Long-context attention, compression & summarization memory, Observe–plan–act agent loops, Conversational assistants & chatbots.
examples: atlas downstream link to Long-context attention, compression & summarization memory
atlas downstream link to Observe–plan–act agent loops
atlas downstream link to Conversational assistants & chatbots
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Sequences, events & time series, Self-attention.

dependencies.rationale: Context windows & working memory stores, selects, or obtains information through Sequences, events & time series.
In this atlas graph, Context windows & working memory has a `uses` dependency on Self-attention because Self-attention is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Context windows & working memory has a `uses` dependency on Differentiable memory read/write because Differentiable memory read/write is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
